proposal.hypothesis: Early capture moves the initial degraded side fall out of the inverted basin. The first candidate then kept injecting pose/rate feedback near upright and hopped; lowering support stiffness in the second candidate made it collapse. Restricting early capture to the first dynamic side entry and latching the ordinary load-bearing stand target without pose-feedback amplification once measured tilt and height are safe should dissipate motion without losing support.
proposal.expectedEffect: Make impact-left-degraded reach a stable recovery handoff without changing the baseline right-degraded retry or any exact/static recovery path, reducing Suite violations with no passing-gate regression.

gateReasons: impact-left-degraded: yaw-overshoot regressed from passing to failing
